201.description: Account created successfully. Returns access and refresh tokens.

400.description: Invalid input - check email format and password strength

post.description:
Authenticate with your email and password.

Response depends on your account's MFA configuration:

**Case 1: No MFA enabled**
- Immediate response with access and refresh tokens
- Use tokens for all subsequent API requests

**Case 2: MFA enabled (TOTP and/or Passkeys)**
- Response contains a challenge and available MFA methods
- Choose to verify with TOTP code OR passkey
- Send your verification to one of these endpoints:
  - POST /api/auth/mfa/challenges/{challengeId}/totp (for 6-digit code)
  - POST /api/auth/mfa/challenges/{challengeId}/passkey (for passkey)
- After successful MFA, you'll receive your tokens

The challenge remains valid for 5 minutes.

200.description: Login successful (no MFA required). Returns access and refresh tokens.

400.description: Invalid password - weak or same as current password

post.description:
Set up Time-based One-Time Password (TOTP) authentication on your account.

This is the first step to enable TOTP. It generates a secret and returns:
- A QR code URI to scan with your authenticator app
- A challenge ID needed for the next step

Recommended authenticators: Apple Password, Google Authenticator, Microsoft Authenticator.

Next: Scan the QR code or enter the secret in your app, then verify with POST /mfa/totp/verify.

200.description: TOTP setup initiated. Returns QR code URI and secret for backup.

post.description:
Complete TOTP setup by verifying a code from your authenticator app.

After initializing TOTP with POST /mfa/totp/enroll:
1. Scan the QR code in your authenticator app
2. Wait for a 6-digit code to appear
3. Send that code here to activate TOTP

Requirements:
- Valid 6-digit code from your authenticator (changes every 30 seconds)
- Challenge ID from the enrollment step (expires after 10 minutes)
- Must be done within ~1 minute of the code appearing

Once confirmed: TOTP will be required at every login as your second factor.

204.description: TOTP successfully enabled. It will be required for future logins.

401.description: Invalid TOTP code (incorrect 6-digit code) or expired challenge (enrollment expired)

delete.description: Remove TOTP authentication from your account.

get.description: View all passkeys registered to your account.

200.description: Array of all passkeys registered to this account

get.summary: List your passkeys

post.description:
Begin the process of registering a new passkey.

Workflow:
1. Call this endpoint to get WebAuthn registration options
2. Your device will prompt you to authenticate (touch, face, PIN, or button press)
3. Send the response to POST /mfa/passkeys/verify to complete registration

Optional: Give your passkey a friendly name like "Work YubiKey" or "iPhone Face ID" to easily identify it later.

200.description: WebAuthn registration options - ready to use with your authenticator

post.description:
Finalize passkey registration by verifying your authenticator's response.

After calling POST /mfa/passkeys/options and authenticating with your device:
1. Your device provides a signed response
2. Send that response here to register the credential
3. The server verifies it's legitimate and stores your public key

Response: Your updated list of all passkeys including this new one.

201.description: Passkey registered! Returns list of all your passkeys.

400.description: Registration failed - verification error or invalid response

delete.description: Delete a passkey from your account.

post.description:
Finish logging in by providing your 6-digit authenticator code.

When you log in with email/password and TOTP is enabled, you receive a challenge. Complete it by:
1. Finding the 6-digit code in your authenticator app
2. Sending it here with the challenge ID

Success: You'll receive access and refresh tokens to use the API.

200.description: Login successful. Use tokens for API requests.

400.description: TOTP is not enabled for this challenge (challenge requires passkey instead)

401.description: Invalid TOTP code, missing/expired challenge, or TOTP not configured for user

post.description:
Finish logging in by authenticating with your passkey.

When you log in with email/password and passkeys are enabled, you receive a challenge. Complete it by:
1. Using your browser's WebAuthn API with the provided options
2. Authenticating with your device (touch key, biometric, PIN, etc.)
3. Sending the signed assertion response here

Success: You'll receive access and refresh tokens to use the API.

200.description: Login successful. Use tokens for API requests.

400.description: Passkey authentication is not available for this challenge (challenge requires TOTP instead)

401.description: Invalid/unknown passkey, assertion verification failed, or challenge expired/not found

post.description:
Obtain a fresh access token using your refresh token.

Access tokens expire regularly for security. When yours expires, use this endpoint to get a new one without logging in again.

200.description: Token refresh successful. Returns new access token.

post.description:
This endpoint allows users to create a webhook for monitoring instance status changes.
The webhook will trigger notifications when an instance goes up or down.

Webhooks use the same retry logic as data change notifications, with exponential backoff up to 10 retries in case of delivery failures.

201.description: The webhook has been successfully created.

info.title: Klardaten DATEVconnect Gateway
info.description:
The Klardaten DATEVconnect Gateway provides access to DATEV on-premise systems via DATEVconnect.

**Looking for advanced features?** Check out our [Enterprise API](/docs/enterprise) for pagination, filtering, and enhanced webhook capabilities.

### Key Features

- **Simple Authentication**: Basic user registration, login, and token management
- **DATEVconnect Proxy**: Direct access to DATEV services through our gateway
- **Basic Webhooks**: Get notified of data changes
- **Client Instance Management**: Connect and manage multiple DATEV systems

### Getting Started

Most endpoints require authentication. Here's the basic flow:

1. **Register** or **Login** to get an access token
2. Include the token in all requests: `Authorization: Bearer <your-token>`
3. **Refresh** your token when it expires

#### Quick Example
```http
POST https://api.klardaten.com/api/auth/login
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "email": "your-email@example.com",
  "password": "your-password"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "access_token": "eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...",
  "refresh_token": "dGhpcyBpcyBhIHJlZnJlc2ggdG9rZW4..."
}
```

Use the `access_token` in subsequent requests:
```http
GET https://api.klardaten.com/api/client-instances
Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9...
```

### Fixed API keys
For machine-to-machine usage, you can also create and use API keys and send them as `Authorization: Bearer uk-...` instead of a user access token. Create them via our dashboard or by calling `POST https://api.klardaten.com/api/users/:userid/api-keys`.

### Connecting to DATEV Systems

To access a DATEV system through our API, you need to create a client instance with the consultant number (Beraternummer):

#### 1. Create a Client Instance
```http
POST https://api.klardaten.com/api/client-instances
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "name": "My DATEV instance",
  "consultant_number": "7654321"
}
```

Response:
```json
{
  "id": "48fa2446-c731-4a74-9ea7-b4bd6d66fe5f",
  "name": "My DATEV instance", 
  "consultant_number": "7654321"
}
```

#### 2. Use the Instance ID in Requests
Include the instance ID in the `x-client-instance-id` header for all DATEV-related requests:

```http
GET https://api.klardaten.com/datevconnect/master-data/v1/clients
Authorization: Bearer <your-token>
x-client-instance-id: 48fa2446-c731-4a74-9ea7-b4bd6d66fe5f
```

#### 3. Manage Your Instances
- **List all instances**: `GET /api/client-instances`
- **Get instance details**: `GET /api/client-instances/{instance-id}`

> **Note**: Instance creation can be done before or after installing the Klardaten Connector on your DATEV system.

### Data Changes Webhooks

Get notified when DATEV data changes. Create webhooks using `POST /api/data-changes/webhooks`.

#### Example Payload
```json
{
  "id": "55571da2-1223-4b12-bdd4-88e8e38b77aa",
  "instance_id": "2036d556-9b1f-498f-b2e2-0b6d91fa8d73",
  "timestamp": 1720615358,
  "current": [
    {
      "client_id": "626029d4-dc00-4f14-9036-2170a5694448",
      "client_number": 10000,
      "client_status": "1", 
      "client_name": "Franz Camembert",
      ...
    }
  ],
  "type": "update"
}
```

#### Supported Entities
- `client`
- `document`


### Webhook Delivery and Retry Logic

When a webhook event occurs, our system sends a webhook to the specified endpoint. If the webhook delivery fails, our system implements a retry mechanism to ensure successful delivery.

#### Key Features of Webhook Retry Logic:

- **Automatic Retries**: If the webhook delivery fails (due to a non-2xx response or a timeout), the system will automatically retry.
- **Exponential Backoff**: The retry intervals increase exponentially after each failed attempt, starting with a 1-second delay for the first retry, doubling with each subsequent retry (e.g., 1s, 2s, 4s, etc.).
- **Max Retry Attempts**: By default, the system will retry up to 10 times. If all retries fail, the webhook delivery will be marked as failed, and the issue will be logged.
- **Timeout Management**: Each webhook attempt is given a maximum of 5 seconds to complete. If no response is received within that time, the attempt will be considered failed.
- **Failure Logging**: Each failed attempt is logged, and after all retries are exhausted, a final failure report will be created.

### Verifying Webhooks Authenticity

We provide an additional security measure to verify the webhooks you receive are genuine. This can be useful if you're looking to ensure only Klardaten webhooks are being made to your endpoint and ensure the information is genuine and as the system expects. These signatures will also help prevent against replay attacks. Verifying the signing secret is optional.

Webhook requests will contain a header which can be used to verify the request's authenticity: `x-klardaten-webhook-signature`. This is used in conjunction with the payload of the request.

#### Getting a Webhook's Secret

When creating a new webhook, a `secret` property will be returned along with the webhook's details.

You should store this secret at your end if you are interested in verifying our webhooks signatures.

#### Verifying the Signature

To verify the webhook signature sent as the `x-klardaten-webhook-signature` header, you should sign the request's body with the webhook secret key using SHA256, then generate a hexadecimal digest, which can be represented as `hexdigest(HMACSHA256(BODY))`.

#### Verification Example (Node.js)

```javascript
const crypto = require("crypto");

function verifyWebhookSignature(body, signature, secret) {
  const hmac = crypto.createHmac('sha256', secret);
  const computedSignature = hmac
    .update(Buffer.from(JSON.stringify(body), 'utf8'))
    .digest('hex');
  
  return computedSignature === signature;
}

// Usage in your webhook handler
const isValid = verifyWebhookSignature(
  req.body, 
  req.headers["x-klardaten-webhook-signature"], 
  "your-webhook-secret"
);
```

### Request Correlation ID

To help with tracking and debugging, our API supports a correlation id mechanism. This allows both the client and server to uniquely identify and trace each request.

How It Works:

- **Client-Supplied Correlation ID**: You can include an optional X-Correlation-Id header in your requests. This ID will be included in the response headers as well, making it easy to track the request across different services or when reporting issues.
- **Auto-Generated Correlation ID**: If you don't provide a X-Correlation-Id in your request, our API will generate a unique correlation id for you and include it in the response headers.
- **Error Reporting**: The Correlation ID is also logged with any errors, making it easy to reference and investigate specific issues. Clients can use this ID when reporting problems, allowing for more efficient troubleshooting.

#### Example

Here's how you can include a X-Correlation-Id in your requests:

```http
GET https://api.klardaten.com/api/client-instances
Authorization: Bearer ey...
X-Correlation-Id: 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
```

In this example, the server will return the same X-Correlation-Id in the response:

```http
HTTP/1.1 200 OK
X-Correlation-Id: 123e4567-e89b-12d3-a456-426614174000
```


### Profiles

A **profile** is a named credential set that authenticates a request against a DATEV instance. Each profile stores a Windows username, an optional domain, and a password under a developer-chosen identifier (`profile_id`). When a DATEVconnect request is made, the profile's credentials are used to authenticate with the DATEV installation on-site.

> **Credentials are never stored at Klardaten.** When you register a profile, the credentials are transmitted directly to the DATEV instance connector and stored there. Klardaten's backend only retains the `profile_id` string to identify which profile to use — not the username, domain, or password.

There are two built-in profile IDs with conventional meaning:

| `profile_id` | Intended use |
|---|---|
| `service-account` | Shared service credentials used for DATEVconnect API access |
| `system-account` | Internal credentials used by the connector itself; not available for DATEVconnect API calls |

You are free to use any string as a `profile_id` (up to 256 characters); these two are provisioned automatically for every new organization–instance link.

Profiles exist at two scopes:

- **Organization profiles** — shared across all users within an organization. These are provisioned automatically when an organization is linked to an instance.
- **User profiles** — private to a single user. Any user can register additional profiles for themselves via the API.

---

### Listing Profiles for an Instance

To see which profiles are available to you for a given instance, including which one is currently set as the default:

```http
GET /api/client-instances/{instance_id}/profiles
Authorization: Bearer ey...
```

Response:

```json
[
  {
    "id": "7a3c8de7-6d2d-4cf6-9a12-3d0877a6b1f4",
    "profile_id": "service-account",
    "scope": "organization",
    "name": "Acme GmbH",
    "is_default": true
  },
  {
    "id": "2b1f9ac3-4e7d-41a2-bb23-9c1234567890",
    "profile_id": "john-personal",
    "scope": "user",
    "name": "john@example.com",
    "is_default": false
  }
]
```

The `scope` field indicates whether the profile belongs to the organization (`"organization"`) or to the authenticated user (`"user"`). The `is_default` field shows which profile will be used automatically if no `x-profile-id` header is provided.

---

### Creating a User Profile via API

Register a new profile (or update an existing one with the same `profile_id`) for the current user on a specific instance:

```http
POST /api/users/me/profile-id
Authorization: Bearer ey...
x-client-instance-id: {instance_id}
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "profile_id": "john-personal",
  "username": "john.doe",
  "domain": "DOMAIN",
  "password": "s3cr3t"
}
```

| Field | Required | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `profile_id` | Yes | Identifier for this profile (max 256 characters) |
| `username` | Yes | Windows username to authenticate with |
| `domain` | No | Windows domain |
| `password` | Yes | Password for the account |

A successful request returns `204 No Content`. If the credentials cannot be validated against the DATEV instance, a `422 Unprocessable Entity` is returned.

---

### Deleting a User Profile

Remove a previously registered profile:

```http
DELETE /api/users/me/profile-id?profile_id=john-personal
Authorization: Bearer ey...
x-client-instance-id: {instance_id}
```

Returns `204 No Content` on success.

---

### Default Profile per Organization

When an organization is first linked to a DATEV instance, two organization profiles are provisioned automatically: `service-account` (for DATEVconnect API access) and `system-account` (used internally by the connector). The `service-account` profile is set as the **organization default**.

The organization default is the fallback used for any member of the organization who has no user-level default configured. It is managed internally and does not require any setup.

---

### Default Profile per User

When a user is first added to an instance, the `service-account` profile is set as their **user default**. This profile takes precedence over the organization default when a request is made without an explicit `x-profile-id` header.

If you have registered additional profiles for yourself and want to switch which one is used by default, contact your organization administrator to update the default profile for your account.

---

### Using a Profile in Requests

On any DATEVconnect API request, you can specify which profile to use via the `x-profile-id` header:

```http
GET /api/datevconnect/clients
Authorization: Bearer ey...
x-client-instance-id: {instance_id}
x-profile-id: john-personal
```

If `x-profile-id` is omitted, the system resolves the profile automatically using the following priority order:

1. **User default** — the default profile registered for the current user on this instance
2. **Organization default** — the default profile for the user's organization on this instance
3. If neither is available, the request is rejected with `403 Forbidden`

Access is also enforced: you can only reference profile IDs that belong to your organization or your own user account. Attempting to use a profile you have no access to returns `403 Forbidden`.

tags.name: DATEVconnect
tags.description:
Our gateway acts as a reverse proxy for DATEVconnect (DATEV’s official Desktop API). It supports the DATEVconnect modules exposed by your DATEV environment and license.

  DATEV’s documentation is the source of truth for endpoints, parameters, and request/response schemas:
  https://developer.datev.de/en/products (tab Desktop APIs)

  URL mapping:
  - Local DATEVconnect:  http://localhost:58454/datev/api/<DATEVCONNECT_PATH>
  - Via Klardaten:       https://api.klardaten.com/datevconnect/<DATEVCONNECT_PATH>

  Example:
  http://localhost:58454/datev/api/master-data/v1/clients
  →
  https://api.klardaten.com/datevconnect/master-data/v1/clients

  The gateway forwards the HTTP method, path, query string, and body (and relevant headers) to DATEVconnect.

name.description: Optional display name of the user

access_token_expires_in.description: Access token expiration time in seconds

refresh_token_expires_in.description: Refresh token expiration time in seconds

current_password.description: Current password of the authenticated user

new_password.description: New password for the authenticated user

challenge_id.description: Identifier required when confirming the TOTP enrollment.

uri.description: otpauth URI that can be encoded as QR code in clients.

challenge_id.description: Enrollment challenge identifier received when starting the TOTP flow.

friendlyName.description: Optional label for the new passkey.

publicKey.description: PublicKeyCredentialCreationOptionsJSON payload to be used for navigator.credentials.create (wrap in { publicKey: ... }).

challenge.description: Challenge returned inside the registration options.

friendlyName.description: Optional friendly name to attach to the passkey.

organization.description: Organization the user belongs to, if any

is_enabled.description: Whether the user account is enabled

password.description: Initial password for the new user

name.description: Updated display name for the user

name.description: Optional display name of the API key

last_used_at.description: Timestamp of the latest successful API key authentication

name.description: Optional display name for the API key

features.description: Complete list of feature flags that should remain enabled for the user

restricted.description: Whether this organization user is limited to explicitly assigned instances.

instance_ids.description: Client instance IDs assigned to the user when restricted.

name.description: Organization-specific display name for the instance

is_test.description: Whether the organization marks the instance as a test instance

is_current.description: Whether this organization is currently active

organization_id.description: Organization the user should switch to

ip_whitelist.description: Replace the organization IP whitelist. Empty or omitted allows all IPs.

name.description: The name of the client instance

consultant_number.description: The consultant number (beraternummer) of the client instance

it_partner_id.description: Optional IT Partner identifier. If omitted, the default IT Partner is used.

instance.description: Service status of the client instance

owner_name.description: Owner name reported by the client instance

it_partner_id.description: Identifier of the assigned IT partner

connection_type.description: Connection type for the client instance

status.description: Service status including instance and other services like datevConnect

last_reply.description: Last reply from the client instance

is_test.description: Whether this is a test instance

success.description: Indicates whether the user was configured successfully

message.description: Optional message describing why the operation did not run

credential_url.description: Short-lived credential URL containing an opaque encrypted token.

expires_at.description: UTC expiry timestamp for the token embedded in the URL.

consultant_number.description: The consultant number (beraternummer) of the client instance

owner.description: The owner of the client instance

it_partner_id.description: Optional IT Partner identifier to associate with the instance

profile_id.description: Profile identifier used when selecting the profile

is_default.description: Whether this profile is configured as the default

created_at.description: The creation date of the webhook

name.description: The name of the IT Partner

size.description: The size of the file in bytes

last_modified.description: The date the file was last modified

timestamp.description: The timestamp of the log entry

available_vas_mb.description: Available VAS memory of the database

db_cpu_usage.description: Database CPU usage of the instance

db_memory_usage.description: Database memory usage of the instance

db_io_stats.description: Database IO usage of the instance
